blurb: CMA foundation-models and broader AI competition-policy work. Launch coverage uses primary GOV.UK CMA case material.

hiring.note: Broader CMA AI work is not a separate hiring entity; use parent CMA/Civil Service recruitment source.

publications.title: CMA Annual Plan 2025 to 2026
publications.summary: The CMA's 2025/26 Annual Plan sets its programme of work, now where its ongoing AI/foundation-models oversight lives after the initial review was embedded into general monitoring: horizon-scanning of emerging markets including foundation models, and use of new Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers (DMCC) Act powers over the digital economy. Reaffirms the CMA's published foundation-model principles as the guide for these markets.

publications.title: AI Foundation Models: Update Paper
publications.summary: CMA’s foundation-models case page hosts the initial review and April 2024 Update Paper, setting out its evolving competition-policy view of foundation-model markets, partnerships and possible regulation.

publications.title: AI Foundation Models: Update paper
publications.summary: CMA update paper confirms final competition and consumer-protection principles for foundation-model markets and identifies risks to fair, open and effective competition.

publications.title: CMA outlines growing concerns in markets for AI Foundation Models
publications.summary: CMA press notice says the regulator has moved from curiosity to “real concerns” about foundation-model market concentration and access points.
